cwe: CWE-770 - Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling
CWE-920 - Improper Restriction of Power Consumption

threat.title: AWS Lambda Function Timeout Abuse and Concurrent Execution Exhaustion
threat.description: Attackers exploit AWS Lambda functions by triggering computationally expensive operations or deploying cryptocurrency mining malware, causing extended execution times and concurrent execution exhaustion. This results in account-level throttling, service degradation, and massive cost overruns (denial of wallet). Vulnerable functions without reserved concurrency limits or proper timeout configurations can scale to thousands of concurrent executions, consuming the account's entire concurrency pool.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker discovers exposed Lambda function URL or API Gateway endpoint through reconnaissance or leaked IAM credentials
2. Identifies function with long timeout (e.g., 15 minutes) and no reserved concurrency limits
3. Deploys cryptocurrency mining malware (e.g., Denonia with XMRig) or triggers expensive operations (large file processing, database queries, external API calls)
4. Floods function with concurrent invocations to reach account-level concurrency limit (default 1,000)
5. Legitimate functions across the AWS account get throttled with 'TooManyRequestsException' errors
6. Attack continues for hours or days, resulting in service outages and bills ranging from thousands to hundreds of thousands of dollars
7. Attacker may use DNS-over-HTTPS (DoH) to hide command-and-control traffic within normal AWS network patterns
prerequisites: Lambda function exposed via Function URL, API Gateway, or event source without authentication
Missing or inadequate reserved concurrency configuration
Long timeout values (5-15 minutes) enabling sustained attacks
Compromised IAM credentials with lambda:InvokeFunction permissions
No CloudWatch alarms for concurrent executions or cost anomalies
Function performs CPU-intensive operations or makes slow external API calls

cve_references.description: No specific CVE assigned - architectural exploitation pattern

real_world_breaches.incident: Denonia - First AWS Lambda Crypto Mining Malware
real_world_breaches.impact: First malware specifically designed for AWS Lambda environments; XMRig-based Monero miner using DNS-over-HTTPS for C2 communication; potential for unlimited concurrent execution abuse
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Compromised AWS Access Keys allowing manual deployment of Go-based 64-bit ELF malware into Lambda environments without concurrency controls

real_world_breaches.incident: Large-Scale AWS Crypto Mining Campaign via Compromised IAM
real_world_breaches.impact: GuardDuty detected campaign targeting EC2, ECS, and Lambda; miners operational within 10 minutes of initial access; massive compute resource consumption and cost overruns
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Stolen IAM credentials with broad permissions; lack of cost anomaly detection and concurrent execution monitoring

real_world_breaches.impact: Multi-week crypto mining operation leveraging serverless auto-scaling; functions cloned to thousands of concurrent executions; cost escalation into tens of thousands of dollars
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Missing reserved concurrency limits; inadequate CloudWatch monitoring; delayed billing anomaly detection

bug_bounty_reports.summary: Lambda function DoS via concurrent execution exhaustion affecting all account functions

security_research.title: Gaining Persistency on Vulnerable Lambdas
security_research.key_finding: Lambda auto-scaling enables attackers to turn one vulnerable function into thousands of compute instances; serverless functions present easy targets due to lack of concurrency controls

security_research.title: Lambda Threat - Best Practices for Lambda Security (MITRE ATT&CK)
security_research.key_finding: Resource hijacking (T1496) is primary threat vector; functions can be exploited for crypto mining with auto-scaling multiplying mining capacity exponentially

security_research.title: AWS Lambda Risks: Concurrency and Timeout Exploitation
security_research.key_finding: Default 1,000 concurrent execution limit can be exhausted by single vulnerable function; reserved concurrency is critical defense mechanism

mitigations.title: Implement Reserved Concurrency Limits
mitigations.description: Configure reserved concurrency at the function level to limit maximum concurrent executions and prevent account-wide throttling. This isolates blast radius and ensures one compromised function cannot exhaust the entire account's concurrency pool.
iso27001_controls: A.17.2.1 - Availability of information processing facilities
nist_csf: PR.DS-4 - Adequate capacity to ensure availability
DE.AE-1 - Baseline of network operations established
validation_steps: Set reserved concurrency to appropriate value based on function criticality (5-50 for most functions)
Verify account-level concurrency is not fully allocated (keep 200+ unreserved)
Test function behavior under throttling conditions (should return 429 TooManyRequestsException)
Validate other functions remain operational when one is throttled

mitigations.title: Optimize Timeout Configuration and Add Internal Circuit Breakers
mitigations.description: Reduce function timeout values to minimum required duration and implement application-level timeout guards. Most API functions should complete in under 30 seconds; batch processing functions should have explicit retry logic with exponential backoff.
nist_csf: PR.DS-4 - Adequate capacity to ensure availability
DE.CM-1 - Network monitored to detect anomalies
validation_steps: Set Lambda timeout to realistic minimum (API: 10-30s, Batch: 1-5min)
Implement internal timeout guard at 85-90% of Lambda timeout
Test function behavior when timeout is reached (graceful degradation)
Monitor Duration and Timeout CloudWatch metrics
Validate error responses include retry guidance

mitigations.title: Deploy Cost Anomaly Detection and GuardDuty Lambda Protection
mitigations.description: Enable AWS Cost Anomaly Detection with machine learning-based thresholds and Amazon GuardDuty Lambda Protection to detect cryptocurrency mining, unusual network activity, and concurrent execution spikes. Configure real-time alerting via SNS and EventBridge.
iso27001_controls: A.16.1.4 - Assessment of information security events
nist_csf: DE.AE-3 - Event data are aggregated and correlated
validation_steps: Enable GuardDuty Lambda Protection in all regions
Configure Cost Anomaly Detection with Lambda service filter
Set SNS alerts to Lambda cost > $100/day threshold
Create EventBridge rule for GuardDuty crypto mining findings
Test alerts by simulating high concurrency (load testing)
Verify alert delivery to security team within 5 minutes
Document incident response procedures for Lambda abuse

indicators: Concurrent executions consistently near account limit (800-1000)
Functions running at or near maximum timeout duration (e.g., 14m 59s)
Unexpected spike in Lambda invocation count (10x-100x baseline)
CloudWatch logs showing DNS-over-HTTPS connections to crypto mining pools
High throttle rate (TooManyRequestsException) across multiple functions
AWS bill anomalies: Lambda compute charges 5x-50x higher than baseline
Network traffic to known mining pool IPs or domains (e.g., xmr-pool.com)
Function code modifications not matching approved deployments

log_queries.query:
# Detect timeout patterns and long-running executions
fields @timestamp, @message, @duration, @requestId
| filter @type = "REPORT"
| filter @duration > 25000  # Functions running > 25 seconds
| stats count() as execution_count, 
        avg(@duration) as avg_duration,
        max(@duration) as max_duration,
        max(@maxMemoryUsed) as peak_memory 
  by @functionName
| filter execution_count > 50  # High frequency pattern
| sort execution_count desc

# Detect concurrent execution spikes and throttling
fields @timestamp, @message
| filter @message like /TooManyRequestsException/
  or @message like /Rate exceeded/
  or @message like /ReservedConcurrentExecutions/
| stats count() as throttle_count by bin(5m) as time_window
| filter throttle_count > 10
| sort time_window desc

-- Detect suspicious Lambda function updates and concurrency changes
SELECT 
  eventtime,
  useridentity.principalid,
  requestparameters.functionname,
  requestparameters.code,
  requestparameters.environment,
  requestparameters.timeout,
  sourceipaddress,
  useragent
FROM cloudtrail_logs
WHERE eventsource = 'lambda.amazonaws.com'
  AND eventname IN (
    'UpdateFunctionCode20150331v2',
    'UpdateFunctionConfiguration20150331v2',
    'PutFunctionConcurrency20170405v2',
    'CreateFunction20150331'
  )
  AND year = '2026' AND month = '01'
  AND (
    requestparameters.timeout > 300  -- Timeout > 5 minutes
    OR requestparameters.reservedconcurrentexecutions > 100
  )
ORDER BY eventtime DESC
LIMIT 100;

index=aws_cloudwatch sourcetype="aws:cloudwatch:log" 
| spath output=duration path=duration
| spath output=memory path=maxMemoryUsed  
| spath output=billed_duration path=billedDuration
| where duration > 25000
| stats count as execution_count, 
        avg(duration) as avg_duration,
        max(duration) as max_duration,
        sum(billed_duration) as total_billed_ms
  by functionName
| where execution_count > 100
| eval total_billed_hours = total_billed_ms / (1000 * 60 * 60)
| eval estimated_cost_usd = total_billed_hours * 0.0000166667  
| where estimated_cost_usd > 50
| sort -estimated_cost_usd

event.provider: "lambda.amazonaws.com" 
AND (event.action: "UpdateFunctionCode" OR event.action: "UpdateFunctionConfiguration")
AND aws.cloudtrail.request_parameters.timeout: > 300
| stats count() by aws.cloudtrail.user_identity.principal_id, 
                  aws.cloudtrail.request_parameters.function_name,
                  source.ip

tools.name: AWS CloudWatch ServiceLens
tools.usage: Visualize Lambda performance, timeouts, and concurrent execution patterns

tools.name: AWS Cost Explorer
tools.usage: Daily Lambda cost monitoring; set alerts for costs > $100/day anomaly threshold

tools.name: Amazon GuardDuty
tools.usage: Real-time detection of crypto mining and malicious network activity
